Public Works Commission Meeting 07-28-21 Item 4.A.

Public Works Commission Agenda Report

To: Chair Simmens and Members of the Public Works Commission Prepared by: Troy Spayd, Assistant Public Works Director/City Engineer Approved by: Rob DuBoux, Public Works Director/City Engineer Date prepared:   July 21, 2021 Meeting date: July 28, 2021 Subject: Capital Improvement Projects and Disaster Recovery Projects Status Report

6df350e4-624d-47a3-8073-949132ba0407.label: text_chunk
6df350e4-624d-47a3-8073-949132ba0407.text: RECOMMENDED ACTION: Receive and file report on the status of the City’s current and upcoming Capital Improvements Projects and Disaster Recovery Projects.

07cf076c-af75-42f9-b723-56663617da5c.text:

p Malibu Park Drainage Improvements

¾ Project  Description:

  This  project  consists  of  storm  drain  repairs  within  the Malibu Park neighborhood. These repairs are the result of damage caused by the subsequent storms after the Woolsey Fire.

Work will include clearing and grubbing, demolition, cold milling, removal of existing corrugated metal pipe, new asphalt berms/dikes, pavement repairs, pavement overlay, striping, new reinforced concrete pipes and rock slope protection.

¾ Updates:

  Staff  continues  to  work  with  the  contractor  reviewing  project submittals.

0bc469c2-ed74-42c5-9449-f167f839880c.text:
Disaster Projects (Not Started)

Clover Heights Storm Drain (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project consists of design and construction of a few storm drains on Clover Heights.  The existing storm drain facility ends at the intersection  of  Clover  Heights  and  Harvester  Road.    During  storms,  this intersection floods and causes debris to block the road.  An extended storm drain towards the end of Clover Heights will help eliminate this issue.

¾ Project Description:

  This project consists of repairing the existing storm drain on Latigo Canyon Road located approximately 2,500 feet from PCH that was damaged by the Woolsey Fire.

¾ Project Description:

  This project will be funded through a grant from FEMA after the Woolsey Fire.  The City will create a complete inventory of storm drains,  culverts,  debris  basins,  manholes,  and  other  drainage  structures within the City.

1. **Malibu Park Drainage Improvements**: This project was initiated as a direct response to the damage inflicted by the Woolsey Fire, emphasizing the need for improved drainage systems to mitigate future risks.

In summary, both the Malibu Park Drainage Improvements and the Guardrail Replacement Citywide projects are critical responses to the destruction caused by the Woolsey Fire, underscoring the fire's significant impact on local infrastructure and the community's efforts to recover and improve safety measures.
